author: Dr. Olivia Reyes (Emergency Medicine) / Dr. Daniel Okafor (Medicine PGY-2) / Dr. Marisol Vega (Medicine attending)
content:
ADMISSION HISTORY AND PHYSICAL — UNIVERSITY OF MARYLAND MEDICAL CENTER
Date/Time: 7/6/2027, 5:30 PM (ED arrival 8:15 AM)
Patient: Bell, Marcus D (MRN: MRN-90216044)
DOB: 11/22/1983 (Age 43)

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Pleuritic right-sided chest pain and shortness of breath.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr. Bell is a previously healthy 43-year-old construction foreman who presents with 6 hours of dyspnea and sharp, pleuritic RIGHT-sided chest pain that began suddenly this morning. He notes a mild dry cough and a brief episode of dizziness. He denies recent trauma, hemoptysis, fever, calf pain or swelling that he had noticed, or prior blood clots.

IMPORTANT — provoking factors: He has had NO recent surgery, hospitalization, long-haul travel, prolonged immobilization, or known prior VTE. He is on no medications and does not take hormones. This makes the event essentially UNPROVOKED.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: None significant. Overweight (BMI ~29). No liver disease, no known malignancy.
PAST SURGICAL HISTORY: None.
MEDICATIONS: None (occasional OTC multivitamin).
ALLERGIES: NKDA.
FAMILY HISTORY: Father with colorectal cancer (treated, in remission) at 62; maternal grandfather reportedly had a leg clot. (See family history.)
SOCIAL HISTORY: Construction foreman ~20 years. Former smoker (~6 pack-years, quit 2014). Social alcohol. Married, two children; primary breadwinner. (See social history.)

ED COURSE / VITALS:
Arrival: T 98.7, HR 96, BP 128/82, RR 20, SpO2 94% RA. Hemodynamically stable; mild tachycardia.
- Labs (LAB001): WBC 11.8, normal LDH and uric acid, troponin NEGATIVE, BNP normal, D-dimer markedly elevated, normal renal and hepatic function, baseline coags.
- CTPA (IMG001): SEGMENTAL pulmonary embolus (right lower lobe), normal RV:LV ratio. INCIDENTAL heterogeneous enhancement in the right hepatic lobe — favored focal fatty infiltration but malignancy not excluded; dedicated liver imaging recommended.
- Right LE venous duplex (IMG002): extensive DVT (right proximal femoral, posterior tibial, greater saphenous).
- TTE (IMG003): normal RV size/function — no right-heart strain.
- Anticoagulation started in the ED with a heparin infusion.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Well-appearing, mildly anxious adult male in no acute distress at rest.
CV: Tachycardic, regular; no murmur/rub/gallop. No JVD.
Lungs: Clear; mild pain with deep inspiration on the right.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender; no hepatomegaly appreciated; no palpable mass.
Extremities: Right calf mildly fuller than left, non-tender today; no cords; left leg normal.
Neuro: Intact.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
43M, previously healthy, with acute unprovoked SEGMENTAL PE + extensive RIGHT-leg DVT (low-risk PE: troponin negative, no RV strain), and an incidentally discovered liver abnormality that requires characterization.
#VTE — start therapeutic anticoagulation (heparin infusion initially); risk-stratify (done: low-risk). Admit for further workup of the liver lesion and anticoagulation management.
#Liver lesion — dedicated MRI; involve hematology and, as needed, hepatology/IR; the unprovoked VTE plus a liver mass raises concern for occult malignancy.
#Disposition — admit to medicine.
Code status: Full. Verbal proxy: wife.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Olivia Reyes, MD (Emergency Medicine); Dr. Daniel Okafor, MD (PGY-2); Dr. Marisol Vega, MD (attending). 7/6/2027.
